title: Spreadsheets, Python, Reproducibility, and Calculation Checks · Worked Case: Spreadsheet Validation
summary: Turn a hand calculation into a reusable, tested worksheet and small Python model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math beg spreadsheet validation increment in a reproducible calculation tool with test cases. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Add input constraints, units, labels, and audit cells to a spreadsheet model.
Use relative and absolute cell references intentionally in copied formulas.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Spreadsheet Validation evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 21 units, changes by −8 units, then by +6 units. Calculate the final quantity.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Preserve the signs and compute 21 − 8 + 6; compare with the starting scale.
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units.
